llbacks (redirected from Main.GDPJSCallbacks ) The GDP is no longer supported by the Accessors Framework. This page exists as a historical reference. Problem Statement We want to use callbacks so that the GDPLogSubscriber accessor is a spontaneous a

t the GDPLogSubscriber accessor is a spontaneous accessor. Under Cape Code, we now have a spontaneous accessor. Under Node, we were using polling, where the accessor had an input. However, now, under Node, the GDPLogSubscriber is not generating outp

Node, the GDPLogSubscriber is not generating output. How to run what we have To run the Cape Code version: $PTII/bin/vergil /Users/cxh/ptII/ptolemy/actor/lib/jjs/modules/gdp/test/auto/GDPLogSubscribeJS.xml To run the Node Host Composite Accessor ver

JS.xml To run the Node Host Composite Accessor version: (cd /Users/cxh/ptII/org/terraswarm/accessor/accessors/web/gdp/test/auto; node ../../../hosts/node/nodeHostInvoke.js -timeout 12000 gdp/test/auto/GDPLogSubscribeJS) The problem is that the Traina

/GDPLogSubscribeJS) The problem is that the TrainableTest connected to the GDPLogSubscriber does not run: nodeHost.js: invoking wrapup ( ) for accessor: GDPLogSubscribeJS.GDPLogSubscribe2 nodeHost.js: invoking wrapup ( ) for accessor: GDPLogSubscribe

invoking wrapup ( ) for accessor: GDPLogSubscribeJS.GDPLogAppend2 nodeHost.js: while invoking wrapup ( ) of all accessors, an exception was thrown: Error: GDPLogSubscribeJS.TrainableTest2The input handler of this accessor was never invoked. Usually,

ndler of this accessor was never invoked. Usually, this is an error indicating that starvation is occurring. bash-3.2$ [$[Get Code]] What we need When we first call subscribe, we need to register a callback that when invoked, will send data to the ou

llback that when invoked, will send data to the output port of GDPSubscriber. The callback will be invoked by the GDP C code that gets an event that a subscription is present. The callback eventually needs to invoke JavaScript code that sends the dat

needs to invoke JavaScript code that sends the data. So, we need a way to tell the GDP C code to callback to invoke a JavaScript callback. Subscription Files A review of files is in order: $PTII/ptolemy/actor/lib/jjs/modules/gdp/test/auto/GDPLogSubsc

my/actor/lib/jjs/modules/gdp/test/auto/GDPLogSubscribe.xml The Cape Code Model, which runs in vergil $PTII/org/terraswarm/accessor/accessors/web/gdp/test/auto/GDPLogSubscribeJS.js Code generated from GDPLogSubscribe.xml. To run it, use: (cd /Users/c

GDPLogSubscribe.xml. To run it, use: (cd /Users/cxh/ptII/org/terraswarm/accessor/accessors/web/gdp/test/auto; node ../../../hosts/node/nodeHostInvoke.js -timeout 6000 gdp/test/auto/GDPLogSubscribeJS) $PTII/org/terraswarm/accessor/accessors/web/gdp/G

$PTII/org/terraswarm/accessor/accessors/web/gdp/GDPLogSubscribe.js The accessor definition shared between the Cape Code and Node accessor hosts. $PTII/org/terraswarm/accessor/accessors/web/hosts/node/node_modules/@accessors-modules/gdp/gdp.js The gd

/node_modules/@accessors-modules/gdp/gdp.js The gdp module that is found by the Node Accessor Host. $PTII/node_modules/@terraswarm/gdp/gdpjs/gdpjs.js The version of gdpjs.js that is found by Node when Node reads var gdpjs = require('@terraswarm/gdp')

Node reads var gdpjs = require('@terraswarm/gdp'); in gdp.js . Important : gdpjs must be installed with: cd $PTII npm install vendors / gdp / gdp / lang / js [$[Get Code]] $PTII/vendors/gdp/gdp/lang/js/gdpjs/gdpjs.js The master copy of gdpjs.js that

js/gdpjs/gdpjs.js The master copy of gdpjs.js that is read by npm install vendors/gdp/gdp/lang/js . For development and debugging, edit $PTII/node_modules/@terraswarm/gdp/gdpjs/gdpjs.js , and then before running npm install vendors/gdp/gdp/lang/js ,

fore running npm install vendors/gdp/gdp/lang/js , update the master copy with cp $PTII / node_modules /@ terraswarm / gdp / gdpjs / gdpjs.js $PTII / vendors / gdp / gdp / lang / js / gdpjs / gdpjs.js [$[Get Code]] $PTII/node_modules/@terraswarm/gdp/

[$[Get Code]] $PTII/node_modules/@terraswarm/gdp/libs Shared libraries loaded by gdpjs.js . libgdpjs contains some glue code for the interface. Building the GDP In Ptolemy II, the GDP source code is typically installed in $PTII/vendors/gdp/gdp by t

typically installed in $PTII/vendors/gdp/gdp by the GDPManager. One way to get the GDP set up is to run (cd $PTII/ptolemy/actor/lib/jjs/modules/gdp/test; $PTII/bin/ptjacl GDPManager.tcl) Or: cd $PTII / vendors mkdir gdp cd gdp git clone repoman @ r

I / vendors mkdir gdp cd gdp git clone repoman @ repo.eecs.berkeley.edu:projects / swarmlab / gdp.git cd gdp [$[Get Code]] Install Macports . / adm / gdp-setup make all [$[Get Code]] Subscription Callbacks The GDP has C support for callbacks. The GDP

backs The GDP has C support for callbacks. The GDPLogSubscribe and GDPLogRead accessors both eventually call read_gcl_records() , which is defined in gdp/gdp/lang/js/gdpjs/gdpjs.js , and gets installed in node_modules/@terraswarm/gdp/gdpjs/gdpjs.js .

d in node_modules/@terraswarm/gdp/gdpjs/gdpjs.js . ( FIXME: There are a number of different files that are called in part because the code has evolved. Initially, the JavaScript that invokes the GDP C code was a line-for-line port of GDP C utilities

C code was a line-for-line port of GDP C utilities from C to JavaScript. ) read_gcl_records() takes a gdp_event_cbfunc argument, which is passed to do_multiread() , which is also defined in gdpjs.js . In do_multiread() , if we get a GDP_EVENT_DATA ,

In do_multiread() , if we get a GDP_EVENT_DATA , then we invoke the method that was passed as an argument: datum = gdp_datum_new_js ( ) ; ... var crec = 0 ; // counts records read - an index into recarray_out[] ... evtype_int = gdp_event_gettype_js

array_out[] ... evtype_int = gdp_event_gettype_js ( gev_Ptr ) ; //C //C // decode it switch ( evtype_int ) { case GDP_EVENT_DATA : ... if ( gdp_event_cbfunc ) { // TBD1 - cleanup here // do this stashing into recarray_out even if we /

// do this stashing into recarray_out even if we // don't have a non-null gdp_event_cbfunc() // grab record contents for this newly read record var val = gdp_datum_buf_as_string ( datum ) ; // grab record number and timestamp for this newly read reco

cord number and timestamp for this newly read record var ts = gdp_datum_getts_as_string_js ( datum , true /* format */ ) ; // TBD: below check for 64-bit integer return type, gdp_recno_t var rn = gdp_datum_getrecno_js ( datum ) ; // TBD: check that r

datum_getrecno_js ( datum ) ; // TBD: check that recno and rn agree - which to use here? recarray_out [ crec ] = { recno : rn , // for now we use gdp's record number timestamp : ts , value : val } ; crec ++; if ( debug ) { console. log ( 'gdpjs.js: d

rec ++; if ( debug ) { console. log ( 'gdpjs.js: do_multiread(): GDP_EVENT_DATA: about to call gdp_event_cbfunc()' ) ; } // TBD1 - cleanup here gdp_event_cbfunc ( evtype_int , datum , recarray_out ) ; } [$[Get Code]] From the above, the callback meth

} [$[Get Code]] From the above, the callback method should take the following arguments evtype_int - An int, returned by gdp_event_gettype_js(gev_Ptr) . The value here would be GDP_EVENT_DATA. (Note that if we receive a GDP_EVENT_EOS event signify

e that if we receive a GDP_EVENT_EOS event signifying End Of Subscription, we also call gdp_event_cbfunc() and would pass GDP_EVENT_EOS) datum - The datum structure, as returned by gdp_datum_new_js() recarray_out - The crec elepment has the record nu

recarray_out - The crec elepment has the record number, timestamp and value Note that the version of do_multiread() in gdpjs.js is different than the current version in $PTII/vendors/gdp/gdp/apps/gdp-reader.c . In the version in gdp-reader.c , we ha

reader.c . In the version in gdp-reader.c , we have: void multiread_cb ( gdp_event_t * gev ) { ( void ) multiread_print_event ( gev , false ) ; gdp_event_free ( gev ) ; } ... do_multiread ( gdp_gcl_t * gcl , gdp_recno_t firstrec , const char * dtstr

* gcl , gdp_recno_t firstrec , const char * dtstr , int32_t numrecs , bool subscribe , bool use_callbacks ) { ... if ( use_callbacks ) cbfunc = multiread_cb ; ... if ( subscribe ) { ... estat = gdp_gcl_subscribe ( gcl , firstrec , numrecs , NULL , c

cl_subscribe ( gcl , firstrec , numrecs , NULL , cbfunc , NULL ) ; [$[Get Code]] Node ffi Callbacks. Node ffi tutorial - async library calls Add .async to calls Node ffi callback.js - defines ffi.Callback Node ffi test/callback.js - tests for ffi.Cal

back Node ffi test/callback.js - tests for ffi.Callback Node ffi call c lib with callback function (Stackoverflow) The Stackoverflow page is very useful. What we want to do is call gdp_gcl_subscribe() with a callback. In accessors/web/hosts/node/node

with a callback. In accessors/web/hosts/node/node_modules/@accessors-modules/gdp/gdp.js , we have a subscribe() function that passes gdp_event_cbfunc to read_gcl_records() , which is defined in gdpjs.js . Formerly, the code looked like: var gdp_even

.js . Formerly, the code looked like: var gdp_event_cbfunc = null // No callback needed when just logging. [$[Get Code]] Instead, we define an event callback: // Define an event callback. // gdp.h defines: // typedef void (*gdp_event_cbfunc_t)(

ines: // typedef void (*gdp_event_cbfunc_t)( // the callback function // gdp_event_t *ev); // the event triggering the call // FIXME: Copied from gdpjs.sj var ffi = require ( 'ffi' ) ; var ref = require ( 'ref' ) ; var gdp

( 'ffi' ) ; var ref = require ( 'ref' ) ; var gdp_event_t = ref. types . void ; // opaque for us up here in JS var gdp_event_tPtr = ref. refType ( gdp_event_t ) ; // End of copy // Pick one of the next two var statements. //var gdp_event_cbfunc = nu

xt two var statements. //var gdp_event_cbfunc = null // No callback needed when just logging. var gdp_event_cbfunc = ffi. Callback ( 'void' , [ gdp_event_tPtr ] , function ( gdp_event ) { console. log ( "CallBack!!!!!!!!!!!!!!" ) ; } ) ; [$[Get Code]

( "CallBack!!!!!!!!!!!!!!" ) ; } ) ; [$[Get Code]] Unfortunately, it seems like our callback is never called. Also, we probably need to change getNextData() , which is used for polling, and have it not try to get data. Subscription Analysis I have s

ot try to get data. Subscription Analysis I have some very ugly example code created by someone else. The idea, which was not a bad idea, was to rewrite the GDP C code as JavaScript. We ended up with a read_gcl_records() JavaScript method that takes

a read_gcl_records() JavaScript method that takes a bunch of args and tries to do too much. That method is used in gdp.js and defined in gdpjs.js. Here's what happens in the Subscription Accessor at $PTII/org/terraswarm/accessor/accessors/web/gdp/G

$PTII/org/terraswarm/accessor/accessors/web/gdp/GDPLogSubscribe.js : Initialize registers a handler for logname that closes an open subscription and opens a new one: /** Add an input handler that will subscribe to a log. */ exports. initialize = fun

l subscribe to a log. */ exports. initialize = function ( ) { var self = this ; // Set an input handler to unsubscribe and then invoke this initialize() // function when a new logname is provided. this . addInputHandler ( 'logname' , function ( ) { /

s . addInputHandler ( 'logname' , function ( ) { // If there is an open subscription, close it. self. exports . wrapup . call ( self ) ; // Open the new subscription. self. exports . subscribe . call ( self ) ; } ) ; self. exports . subscribe . call

( self ) ; } ) ; self. exports . subscribe . call ( self ) ; } [$[Get Code]] In GDPLogSubscribe.js , subscribe() : /** If a non-empty logname is given, subscribe to the log. */ exports. subscribe = function ( ) { var self = this ; var logname = this

nction ( ) { var self = this ; var logname = this . get ( 'logname' ) ; if ( logname === '' ) { // Nothing more to do. return ; } var logdname = this . get ( 'logdname' ) ; // Create or connect to a log. // The second argument specifies to open the l

og. // The second argument specifies to open the log "read only." log = new GDP. GDP ( logname , 1 , logdname ) ; // Listen for data from the log. log. on ( 'data' , function ( data ) { console. log ( '****** received: ' + data ) ; self. send ( 'data

'****** received: ' + data ) ; self. send ( 'data' , data ) ; console. log ( '****** sent data: ' + data ) ; } ) ; log. setDebugLevel ( this . getParameter ( 'debugLevel' ) ) ; // Subscribe to the log so that 'data' events are emitted. log. subscrib

g so that 'data' events are emitted. log. subscribe ( this . getParameter ( 'startrec' ) , this . getParameter ( 'numrec' ) , this . getParameter ( 'timeout' ) ) ; } ; [$[Get Code]] Focusing on this call log. subscribe ( this . getParameter ( 'startr

all log. subscribe ( this . getParameter ( 'startrec' ) , this . getParameter ( 'numrec' ) , this . getParameter ( 'timeout' ) ) ; [$[Get Code]] Earlier in GDPLogSubscribe.xml, we have var GDP = require ( 'gdp' ) ; ... exports . subscribe = function

re ( 'gdp' ) ; ... exports . subscribe = function ( ) { ... log = new GDP. GDP ( logname , 1 , logdname ) ; [$[Get Code]] For the Node Host, the subscribe() method is defined in $PTII/org/terraswarm/accessor/accessors/web/hosts/node/node_modules/@acc

ccessor/accessors/web/hosts/node/node_modules/@accessors-modules/gdp/gdp.js : /** Subscribe. * @param self The CapeCode subscribe() function passes "this" as its first argument, * so we need to do the same. * @param {int} startrec The starting rec

he same. * @param {int} startrec The starting record. The first record is record 1. * @param {int} numrecs The number of records * @param {int} timeout The timeout in milliseconds. */ exports. GDP . prototype . subscribe = function ( self , startr

. prototype . subscribe = function ( self , startrec , numrecs , timeout ) { // console.log("node_modules/gdp/gdp.js:subscribe(" + startrec + ", " + numrecs + ", " + timeo\ ut + ")" ) ; var gdpd_addr = gdpd_addr ; var gcl_name = this . name var g

ddr = gdpd_addr ; var gcl_name = this . name var gcl_firstrec = startrec ; var gcl_numrecs = numrecs var gcl_subscribe = true ; var gcl_multiread = false ; var conout = true ; // recdest = -1 so output to console.log() var wait_for_events = false ; /

t to console.log() var wait_for_events = false ; // don't wait indefinitely in read_gcl_records() for var gcl_get_next_event = false ; // See {$ACCESSORS_HOME}/wiki/Main/GDPJSCallbacks // and http://stackoverflow.com/questions/36819679/node-fficall-c

tackoverflow.com/questions/36819679/node-fficall-c-lib-with-callback-function // Define an event callback. // gdp.h defines: // typedef void (*gdp_event_cbfunc_t)( // the callback function // gdp_event_t *ev); // the event

gdp_event_t *ev); // the event triggering the call // FIXME: Copied from gdpjs.sj var ffi = require ( 'ffi' ) ; var ref = require ( 'ref' ) ; var gdp_event_t = ref. types . void ; // opaque for us up here in JS var gdp_event_tPtr

// opaque for us up here in JS var gdp_event_tPtr = ref. refType ( gdp_event_t ) ; // End of copy var gdp_event_cbfunc = null // FIXME: not sure about this: no callback needed when just logging //var gdp_event_cbfunc = ffi.Callback('void', [gdp_event
gdp_event_cbfunc = ffi.Callback('void', [gdp_event_tPtr], // function(gdp_event) { // console.log("CallBack!!!!!!!!!!!!!!"); // }); console. log ( "node_modules/gdp/gdp.js

}); console. log ( "node_modules/gdp/gdp.js:subscribe(): about to call read_gcl_records()" ) ; var data = gdpjs. read_gcl_records ( gdpd_addr , gcl_name , gcl_firstrec , gcl_numrecs , gcl_subscribe , gcl_multiread , /*recdest,*/ conout , gdp_e

ribe , gcl_multiread , /*recdest,*/ conout , gdp_event_cbfunc , wait_for_events , gcl_get_next_event ) ; console. log ( "node_modules/gdp/gdp.js:subscribe(): returned from read_gcl_records(). data:" ) ; console. log ( data ) ; //if (data.records.leng

; console. log ( data ) ; //if (data.records.length > 0) { // return data.records[0].value; //} else { // return null; //} } ; [$[Get Code]] This code is pretty gross but it calls read_gcl_records() , which is defined in gdpjs.js . For subscri

rds() , which is defined in gdpjs.js . For subscription, read_gcl_records() calls do_multiread() : if ( gcl_subscribe || gcl_multiread ) { // DEBUG TBD1 // true for reader-test.js; false for gdpREST_server.js if ( ! gcl_get_next_event ) { // gcl_get_

erver.js if ( ! gcl_get_next_event ) { // gcl_get_next_event is false, so we have not yet subscribed to the log. console. log ( 'gdpjs.js: read_gcl_records(): before do_multiread()' ) ; estat = do_multiread ( gcl_Ptr , gcl_firstrec , gcl_numrecs , gc

tiread ( gcl_Ptr , gcl_firstrec , gcl_numrecs , gcl_subscribe , wait_for_events , recarray_out , conout , gdp_event_cbfunc ) ; } else { // gcl_get_next_event is true, so we have already subscribed to the log. console. log ( 'gdpjs.js: read_gcl_record

the log. console. log ( 'gdpjs.js: read_gcl_records(): before do_multiread_inner()' ) ; estat = do_multiread_inner ( gcl_Ptr , gcl_firstrec , gcl_numrecs , gcl_subscribe , wait_for_events , recarray_out , conout , gdp_event_cbfunc , /* Timeout in sec

ut , conout , gdp_event_cbfunc , /* Timeout in seconds */ 1 ) ; } } else { estat = do_simpleread ( gcl_Ptr , gcl_firstrec , gcl_numrecs , recarray_out , conout ) ; } [$[Get Code]] do_multiread() is also defined in gdpjs.js . do_multiread() will set r

so defined in gdpjs.js . do_multiread() will set recarray_out if any events are received. do_multiread() is a bit of a hack. Note that the version in gdpjs.js is out of date when compared to the version in gdp-reader.c . Looking at gdp-reader.c is b

ion in gdp-reader.c . Looking at gdp-reader.c is better than the above code because gdp-reader.c works! In gdp-reader.c , if we are doing subscription, then main() calls estat = do_multiread ( gcl , firstrec , dtstr , numrecs , subscribe , use_callba

irstrec , dtstr , numrecs , subscribe , use_callbacks ) ; [$[Get Code]] /* ** DO_MULTIREAD --- subscribe or multiread ** ** This routine handles calls that return multiple values via the ** event interface. They might incl

** event interface. They might include subscriptions. */ EP_STAT do_multiread ( gdp_gcl_t * gcl , gdp_recno_t firstrec , const char * dtstr , int32_t numrecs , bool subscribe , bool use_callbacks ) { EP_STAT estat ; void ( * cbfunc ) (

_callbacks ) { EP_STAT estat ; void ( * cbfunc ) ( gdp_event_t * ) = NULL ; EP_TIME_SPEC ts ; if ( use_callbacks ) cbfunc = multiread_cb ; // are we reading by record number or by timestamp? if ( dtstr != NULL ) { // timestamp estat = ep_time_parse (

r != NULL ) { // timestamp estat = ep_time_parse ( dtstr , & ts , EP_TIME_USE_LOCALTIME ) ; if ( ! EP_STAT_ISOK ( estat ) ) { fprintf ( stderr , "Cannot convert date/time string \" %s \" \n " , dtstr ) ; return estat ; } } if ( subscribe ) { // start

) ; return estat ; } } if ( subscribe ) { // start up a subscription if ( dtstr == NULL ) estat = gdp_gcl_subscribe ( gcl , firstrec , numrecs , NULL , cbfunc , NULL ) ; else estat = gdp_gcl_subscribe_ts ( gcl , & ts , numrecs , NULL , cbfunc , NULL

_ts ( gcl , & ts , numrecs , NULL , cbfunc , NULL ) ; } else { { // make the flags more user-friendly if ( firstrec == 0 ) firstrec = 1 ; // start up a multiread if ( dtstr == NULL ) estat = gdp_gcl_multiread ( gcl , firstrec , numrecs , cbfunc , NUL

ultiread ( gcl , firstrec , numrecs , cbfunc , NULL ) ; else estat = gdp_gcl_multiread_ts ( gcl , & ts , numrecs , cbfunc , NULL ) ; } // check to make sure the subscribe/multiread succeeded; if not, bail if ( ! EP_STAT_ISOK ( estat ) ) { char ebuf [

bail if ( ! EP_STAT_ISOK ( estat ) ) { char ebuf [ 200 ] ; ep_app_fatal ( "Cannot %s: \n \t %s" , subscribe ? "subscribe" : "multiread" , ep_stat_tostr ( estat , ebuf , sizeof ebuf ) ) ; } // this sleep will allow multiple results to appear before we

ep will allow multiple results to appear before we start reading if ( ep_dbg_test ( Dbg , 100 ) ) ep_time_nanosleep ( 500000000 ) ; //DEBUG: one half second // now start reading the events that will be generated if ( ! use_callbacks ) { for ( ;; ) {

e generated if ( ! use_callbacks ) { for ( ;; ) { // get the next incoming event gdp_event_t * gev = gdp_event_next ( NULL , 0 ) ; // print it estat = print_event ( gev , subscribe ) ; // don't forget to free the event! gdp_event_free ( gev ) ; EP_ST

to free the event! gdp_event_free ( gev ) ; EP_STAT_CHECK ( estat , break ) ; } } else { // hang for an hour waiting for events sleep ( 3600 ) ; } return estat ; } [$[Get Code]] For Subscription, this boils down to void ( * cbfunc ) ( gdp_event_t *

s boils down to void ( * cbfunc ) ( gdp_event_t * ) = NULL ; ... cbfunc = multiread_cb ; ... estat = gdp_gcl_subscribe ( gcl , firstrec , numrecs , NULL , cbfunc , NULL ) ; ... sleep ( 3600 ) ; [$[Get Code]] So, in JavaScript, we should use this simp

Code]] So, in JavaScript, we should use this simple method and avoid the hell of do_multiread() . Now, the data is visible in the callback. accessors/web/hosts/node/node_modules/@accessors-modules/gdp/gdp.js has a subscribe() that now defines the ca

p/gdp.js has a subscribe() that now defines the callback: exports.GDP.prototype.subscribe = function ( self, startrec, numrecs, timeout ) { ... var gdp_event_cbfunc = ffi.Callback ( 'void' , [ gdp_event_tPtr ] , function ( gdp_event ) { console.log (

nt_tPtr ] , function ( gdp_event ) { console.log ( "gdp.js CallBack!!!!!!!!!!!!!!" ) ; console.log ( gdp_event ) ; if ( typeof gdp_event ! == 'undefined' ) { console.log ( "gdp.js callback: gdp_event is defined." ) ; // Here, we want to first get the

is defined." ) ; // Here, we want to first get the datum. var datum = gdpjs.gdp_event_getdatum_js ( gdp_event ) ; console.log ( "gdp.js callback: datum:" ) console.log ( datum ) ; console.log ( "gdp.js callback: about to try to print datum" ) ; gdpjs

s callback: about to try to print datum" ) ; gdpjs.gdp_datum_print_stdout_js ( datum ) ; var data = gdpjs.gdp_datum_buf_as_string ( datum ) ; console.log ( "gdp.js: callback: data is: " ) ; console.log ( data ) ; // Then call _notifyIncoming, which i

g ( data ) ; // Then call _notifyIncoming, which is defined in ptolemy / actor / lib / jjs / modules / gdp / GDP.js // _notifyIncoming ( data ) ; // gdp / gdp / doc / gdp-programmatic-api.html // says that for gdp_gcl_subscribe ( ) , "It is // the re

that for gdp_gcl_subscribe ( ) , "It is // the responsibility of the callback function // to call gdp_event_free(gev)." gdpjs.gdp_event_free_js ( gdp_event ) ; } } ) ; console.log ( "node_modules/gdp/gdp.js:subscribe(): about to call read_gcl_record

/gdp.js:subscribe(): about to call read_gcl_records()" ) ; var data = gdpjs.read_gcl_records ( gdpd_addr, gcl_name, gcl_firstrec, gcl_numrecs, gcl_subscribe, gcl_multiread, /* recdest, */ conout, gdp_event_cbfunc, wait_for_events, gcl_get_next_event

event_cbfunc, wait_for_events, gcl_get_next_event ) ; [$[Get Code]] Here, the problem is that we have the data in the callback method and we want to emit it. In CapeCode, ptolemy/actor/lib/jjs/modules/gdp/GDPHelper.java has a subscribe() method that

s/gdp/GDPHelper.java has a subscribe() method that looks like public void subscribe ( int startRecord, int numberOfRecords, int timeout ) throws GDPException { EP_TIME_SPEC timeoutSpec = null ; if ( timeout != 0 ) { timeoutSpec = new EP_TIME_SPEC ( t

imeout != 0 ) { timeoutSpec = new EP_TIME_SPEC ( timeout / 1000 , 0 , /* nanoseconds */ 0.001f /* accuracy in seconds */ ) ; } // FIXME: We need to cast to a long here because it seems // like passing longs from JavaScript does not work for us. _gcl.

longs from JavaScript does not work for us. _gcl. subscribe ( ( long ) startRecord, numberOfRecords, timeoutSpec ) ; Runnable blocking = new Runnable ( ) { public void run ( ) { while ( _subscribed ) { // Last argument is a timeout in ms. When it ex

{ // Last argument is a timeout in ms. When it expires, if there // is no data, then an empty HashMap is returned. // FIXME: Any way to set the timeout to wait forever? final HashMap < String , Object > gdpEvent = GDP_GCL. get_next_event ( _gcl, 100

t > gdpEvent = GDP_GCL. get_next_event ( _gcl, 10000 ) ; if ( gdpEvent != null ) { if ( gdpEvent. size ( ) > 0 ) { // Issue the response in the director thread. _issueResponse ( ( ) -> { HashMap < String ,Object > result = ( HashMap < String ,Object

ing ,Object > result = ( HashMap < String ,Object > ) gdpEvent. get ( "datum" ) ; System . out . println ( "GDPHelper.subscribe(): about to call _notifyIncoming " + result. toString ( ) ) ; _currentObj. callMember ( "_notifyIncoming" , _datumToData (
